text.title: language-framework-guide Core Rules
text.summary: Six testable rules for mapping projects to canonical stacks and producing an ADR-grade decision.

rule.statement: Map task type to canonical language: Python for backend APIs/data/ML/scripting; TypeScript for frontend, Node.js backend, full-stack React; Go for high-performance services and CLI; Rust for systems/WASM.
rule.rationale: Sticking to canonical pairings reduces ramp-up time and hiring friction.

rule.statement: For solo/founder projects, prefer Python (FastAPI/Django) or TypeScript (Next.js) — broadest LLM training-data coverage, fewer agent mistakes.
rule.rationale: LLM coding agents are strongest in popular ecosystems; obscure choices increase error rate.

rule.statement: Pick framework by project type: Django for full-featured web apps (admin, ORM, migrations); FastAPI for async APIs/microservices; React for complex SPAs; Next.js for full-stack React with SSR/SSG.
rule.rationale: Framework choice constrains downstream methodologies and team productivity.

rule.statement: Use ruff (not black+isort+flake8) for Python; biome or prettier+eslint for JS/TS.
rule.rationale: Tool consolidation reduces config surface area and CI time.

rule.statement: When selecting a stack, output (1) primary recommendation, (2) two alternatives, (3) ADR stub. Never output only a stack name.
rule.rationale: Single-name answers hide tradeoffs and prevent future audits.

rule.statement: Every stack selection for a project lasting > 6 months requires an ADR with Status/Context/Decision/Alternatives/Consequences. Human sign-off before implementation.
rule.rationale: Long-lived choices need durable rationale; ADRs prevent recurring debates.

rule.statement: Output has primary + 2 alternatives + ADR stub; longevity > 3mo; human signed off on > 6mo projects.
rule.rationale: Aggregate conclusion for the positive branch.

rule.statement: POC / one-day script / mandated stack — no decision to record.
rule.rationale: Aggregate conclusion for the negative branch.
